title: Excretions from Private Parts
short_rule: Any normal or abnormal excretion from the front or rear passages breaks ablution.
details: Wudu is nullified by anything that exits from the two openings (penis/vagina, and anus). This includes the regular passing of urine, feces, flatulence, as well as pre-seminal or post-urinary discharges.
actionable_steps: Thoroughly clean the affected area (istinja).
Ensure that the exiting of the substance has completely stopped.
Perform a complete new Wudu.
spiritual_wisdom: Maintaining a state of ritual purity involves both physical cleanliness and spiritual readiness. Renewing your ablution after relieving yourself gently washes away sluggishness and re-centers your mind towards a quiet, focused connection with Allah.
remedy_or_consequence: Must perform Wudu again before engaging in prayer.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Flowing Filth or Bleeding
short_rule: Impure discharges like blood or pus that flow from the body break ablution.
details: According to the Hanafi and Hanbali schools, filth that flows from a wound, such as blood or pus, nullifies Wudu if it is voluminous enough to be repulsive or flows beyond its point of exit to a place that normally must be cleaned.
actionable_steps: Clean the affected skin.
Bandage the wound to stop the active bleeding or discharge.
Perform a new Wudu.
spiritual_wisdom: Our bodies are beautiful trusts from the Creator. When we experience an injury or illness, taking a moment to tend to our wounds and renew our ablution reminds us of Allah's healing power and restores our inner tranquility.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Deep Sleep and Loss of Intellect
short_rule: Losing consciousness through heavy sleep, fainting, or intoxication breaks your ablution.
details: Wudu is invalidated by the loss of intellect through heavy sleep, fainting, drunkenness, or insanity. An exception is often made for light sleep while sitting firmly or standing, as one retains a level of consciousness.
actionable_steps: Awaken fully and regain mental clarity.
Cleanse oneself if any physical emissions occurred during sleep.
Perform a new Wudu.
spiritual_wisdom: True worship requires presence of mind and heart. Renewing your ablution after sleep or fainting gently brings your awareness back to the Divine, ensuring your prayers are offered with full consciousness and sincere devotion.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Direct Contact with Genitals
short_rule: Touching the private parts directly with the hand breaks the ablution.
details: Direct contact with the genitals, such as a man touching his penis or a woman touching her vaginal area with her bare hand, nullifies Wudu.
actionable_steps: Wash the hands if necessary.
Perform a new Wudu before engaging in prayer or reading the Quran.
quran_hadith_evidence: Whoever touches his sexual organ, may not pray until he makes wudu. (Reported by Busrah bint Safwan)
spiritual_wisdom: The boundaries of ritual purity teach us mindfulness about our physical bodies. Renewing ablution after such contact helps us transition back into a state of elevated spiritual focus and reverence for worship.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Lustful Physical Contact
short_rule: Skin-to-skin contact with the opposite gender accompanied by lustful desire nullifies Wudu.
details: Caressing or kissing with normal sensual pleasure, or desiring such pleasure during direct skin-to-skin contact with a woman, breaks the state of ablution.
actionable_steps: Step back and re-center one's intentions.
Perform a new Wudu before praying.
spiritual_wisdom: Prayer is an intimate conversation with the Divine that requires our singular focus. Renewing ablution after sensual moments gently redirects the heart's passions from worldly bonds to heavenly devotion.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Eating Camel Meat
short_rule: Consuming camel meat breaks the ablution according to the Hanbali school.
details: Eating camel meat nullifies Wudu. This is a distinctive position of the Hanbali school, based directly on a specific prophetic command to make Wudu after consuming it.
actionable_steps: Finish the meal.
Wash the hands and mouth to remove food traces.
Perform a new Wudu.
quran_hadith_evidence: He was asked: should we make wudu' after eating camel's meat? He said yes, make wudu' after eating their meat.
spiritual_wisdom: Following specific prophetic guidance, even in dietary matters, fosters a spirit of willing submission. Renewing purity here is a beautiful act of loving obedience to the Prophet's gentle instructions.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Doubt Regarding Purity
short_rule: Merely doubting whether you have broken your ablution is enough to nullify it in the Maliki school.
details: Simply doubting about committing an act which makes one lose pureness is sufficient to break the ablution, requiring a renewal of Wudu. (Note: The Hanbali school holds the opposite view, stating certainty is not negated by doubt).
actionable_steps: Acknowledge the feeling of doubt.
Set aside all uncertainty by proactively performing a fresh Wudu.
spiritual_wisdom: Doubt can weigh heavily on the heart and distract from worship. By renewing ablution at the first sign of doubt, you replace anxiety with the profound peace of certainty before standing before your Lord.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Apostasy from Islam
short_rule: Renouncing Islam immediately breaks one's state of ritual purity.
details: Disbelieving and becoming an apostate after being a Muslim completely breaks the state of minor ablution.
actionable_steps: Sincerely repent and recite the Shahada to return to Islam.
Perform a complete new Wudu (or Ghusl).
spiritual_wisdom: Faith is the internal light that gives our physical purification its true meaning. Returning to the fold of faith and washing anew symbolizes a beautiful spiritual rebirth and an embrace of God's boundless mercy.
remedy_or_consequence: Must return to Islam and perform Wudu.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Vomiting a Mouthful
short_rule: Vomiting an amount equal to a mouthful or more invalidates the ablution.
details: According to the Hanafi school, vomiting an amount equal to a mouthful or more breaks one's Wudu. A mouthful is defined as an amount the mouth cannot withhold without strain.
actionable_steps: Rinse the mouth out thoroughly.
Clean any soiled clothing or areas.
Perform a new Wudu.
spiritual_wisdom: Physical illness can leave us feeling drained and spiritually disconnected. Taking the time to wash and refresh after being sick restores our dignity, bringing physical comfort and a renewed sense of peace.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali
